eGalvanic Deep Bug Verification Report

Curated set of 8 verified, reproducible bugs from deep Playwright testing + targeted security audit. Lower-priority / non-reproducible findings from earlier rounds have been removed at QA lead's direction.
📅 April 20-21, 2026 🌐 acme.qa.egalvanic.ai (V1.21) 👤 abhiyant.singh+admin@egalvanic.com 🖥️ Chromium 136 / 1920×1080 ⏱️ 11 test sessions incl. full 21-page crawl + CRUD + form deep-test + security audit, ~3 hours total
8
Total Verified Bugs
2

5

2
Security Findings
32
PDF Bugs Cross-Checked

Testing Methodology

  • Approach: Deep interactive testing using Playwright browser automation (non-headless Chromium). Each page was visited, forms were opened and submitted, buttons were clicked, validation was tested, and console/network errors were captured.
  • What counts as a bug: Broken functionality, incorrect behavior, missing error handling, silent API failures, UX inconsistencies. NOT included: empty data (environment state), test data pollution, "0 rows" displays.
  • 6 test batches: Rounds 1 & 2 — CRUD, admin, audit log, SLDs, Equipment Library, Issues validation, keyboard a11y. Round 3 (PDF cross-verification) — ran all 32 bugs from Combined Bug Report (March 5, 2026) against live platform. Round 4 (security audit, 2026-04-21) — HTTP header audit on static + API responses, cookie flag audit, client-side storage audit (localStorage/sessionStorage), JWT decode and TTL check, user enumeration probe via login errors, rate-limit probe (6 failed logins), clickjacking iframe PoC, HTTPS redirect verification, password autocomplete audit. Only non-destructive, authorized passive/active probes used — no credentials stuffed, no real accounts targeted, no data modified.
  • Sentry integration observed: The app sends 20+ events per testing session to Sentry (o4509671240105984.ingest.us.sentry.io). Sentry is actively capturing frontend errors — errors flagged in this report are likely also visible in Sentry's issue dashboard.

Pages Tested:

Site Overview (Dashboard)
Tasks
Work Orders
Opportunities
Assets
Connections
Locations
Issues

Work Order Planning
BUG-001
No 404 error page — invalid URLs render blank content area
MEDIUM

Page

Any invalid URL (e.g., /nonexistent-page-xyz-404)

Steps to Reproduce

  1. Log in as admin user
  2. Navigate to any invalid URL, e.g., /nonexistent-page-xyz-404
  3. Observe the page content area

Expected Behavior

A proper 404 "Page Not Found" page should display with a helpful message, a link back to the dashboard, and possibly a search or navigation aid.

Actual Behavior

The page shows the sidebar navigation but the content area is completely blank. No 404 message, no "Page Not Found" text, no redirect. The user has no indication they've reached an invalid URL.

Impact

Users who mistype URLs or follow broken links have no feedback. They see a blank screen and may think the app is broken. This also affects SEO and user trust.

Suggested Fix

Add a catch-all route in React Router that renders a 404 component. The component should show "Page Not Found" message with a "Go to Dashboard" button.

Environment

V1.21, Chromium 136, 1920x1080

Screenshot Evidence

p2-404-page.png — captured 2026-04-21
BUG-002
Content Security Policy blocks Beamer notification fonts — 102+ CSP violations per session
MEDIUM

Page

All pages

Steps to Reproduce

  1. Log in as admin user
  2. Open Browser DevTools (F12) > Console tab
  3. Navigate to any page
  4. Observe CSP violation errors

Expected Behavior

The Content Security Policy should allow Beamer notification widget fonts to load without errors.

Actual Behavior

102 CSP violations logged during a single test session across 12 pages. The font-src directive is set to "'self' https://fonts.gstatic.com data:" which does NOT include "https://app.getbeamer.com". Six different Beamer Lato font files are blocked: Lato-Normal-300a/300b/400a/400b/700a/700b.woff2. Each page load generates 6+ CSP violations.

Impact

Beamer notification widget renders with fallback fonts instead of its intended Lato font family. This causes visual inconsistency in the notification panel. The 102+ console errors per session create noise that obscures real errors during debugging.

Suggested Fix

Update the Content-Security-Policy header to add "https://app.getbeamer.com" to the font-src directive. Change: font-src 'self' https://fonts.gstatic.com data: → font-src 'self' https://fonts.gstatic.com https://app.getbeamer.com data:

Environment

V1.21, Chromium 136, 1920x1080, 102 violations across 12 pages

Screenshot Evidence

p3-dashboard-full.png — captured 2026-04-21
BUG-003
Issue created without required Issue Class — frontend + backend validation gap
MEDIUM

Page

/issues

Steps to Reproduce

  1. Navigate to /issues
  2. Click "+ Create Issue"
  3. Fill Title: "Test Issue Title"
  4. Fill Proposed Resolution: "Test proposed resolution"
  5. Leave Issue Class empty (shows "Select an issue class") — note it is marked required with asterisk *
  6. Click "Create Issue"

Expected Behavior

Validation error should appear for Issue Class (marked required with *). The form should NOT submit without Issue Class.

Actual Behavior

The issue is created successfully — API POST /api/issue/create returns HTTP 201. The form closes, and the issue appears in the list with Issue Class column showing "—" (empty). No validation error is ever shown for the Issue Class field. Both frontend validation AND backend API validation are missing for this required field.

Impact

Issues can be created without classification. This breaks: (1) Filtering/reporting by issue class, (2) NEC violation tracking, (3) Data integrity — existing issues all have "NEC Violation" class, but new ones can be created without any class. (4) The * asterisk marking on Issue Class misleads users into thinking it is enforced.

Suggested Fix

Two fixes needed: (1) Frontend: Add client-side validation for Issue Class dropdown — show "Issue class is required" error when empty on submit. (2) Backend: API should return 400 with validation error when issue_class is null/empty in the POST body.

Environment

V1.21, Chromium 136, 1920x1080, Site: Yxbzjzxj, verified via POST /api/issue/create → 201

Screenshot Evidence

r2p3-issue-submit-no-class.png — captured 2026-04-21
BUG-004
Raw HTTP 400 error + internal API details exposed in UI for invalid asset URLs
HIGH

Page

/assets/:invalidId

Steps to Reproduce

  1. Log in as any user
  2. Navigate to an invalid asset URL, e.g., https://acme.qa.egalvanic.ai/assets/invalid-uuid-test-12345
  3. Wait 4-5 seconds for the page to load

Expected Behavior

A friendly 404 / "Asset not found" page, OR redirect to /assets. Raw backend error messages and HTTP status codes must not be exposed to end users.

Actual Behavior

Page shows raw error banner: "Failed to fetch enriched node details: 400" — leaks both the internal endpoint name ("enriched node details") and the HTTP status code directly to the user. Network tab reveals: `GET /api/api/graph/nodes/invalid-uuid-test-12345/enriched` → 400 `{ "error": "Invalid UUID format" }`. Two problems: (1) the URL has `/api/api/` doubled (URL construction bug in the frontend fetch layer), and (2) the raw backend error is rendered directly in the UI with no sanitization.

Impact

Security: reveals backend API naming ("graph/nodes", "enriched"), URL structure, and even a URL construction bug (`/api/api/`). UX: user stranded with technical error and no recovery. Originally reported March 5, 2026 — still present April 20 (45+ days unfixed).

Suggested Fix

Three fixes: (1) Fix the URL builder in the fetch layer — strip or deduplicate `/api/` prefixes so you cannot produce `/api/api/...`. (2) Add a global API error boundary that catches 4xx/5xx and renders a user-friendly error page (never show raw backend text). (3) For 404-equivalent responses on detail routes, redirect to the list page with a toast.

Environment

V1.21, Chromium 136, 1920x1080, verified 2026-04-20 against live platform. Console+Network tab overlay in screenshot.

Screenshot Evidence

bug006-console-evidence.png — captured 2026-04-21
BUG-005
Issue Title input accepts 2000+ characters — no client-side length limit
LOW

Page

/issues (Create Issue form)

Steps to Reproduce

  1. Navigate to /issues and click "Create Issue"
  2. In the Title field, paste a very long string (e.g., 2000 characters of "A")
  3. Observe no truncation, no warning, no maxLength attribute

Expected Behavior

Title input should either enforce a `maxLength` attribute (common: 200–500 chars) or show a character-counter + warning when approaching the limit. Very long titles break UX on list views and may hit backend column limits causing 500 errors.

Actual Behavior

The input accepts arbitrary length — tested with 2000 characters. No `maxLength` attribute, no counter, no warning. The only limit is whatever the backend enforces (unknown without submission, since other validation gaps may interact).

Impact

Low severity because it is mostly a UX/safety concern, not broken behavior today. But: (1) very long titles break the issues list table (visual overflow), (2) may exceed backend DB column size causing a 500 error on submit, (3) opens a minor DoS vector if combined with bulk automation. Typical issue title is 60–120 chars, so 2000+ is clearly over the intended range.

Suggested Fix

Add `maxLength={250}` (or whatever the backend column size is) to the TextField. Optionally add an `inputProps` helperText showing "X / 250". Keep the validation consistent with backend limits — test that submitting at the limit succeeds and at limit+1 shows a friendly error.

Environment

V1.21, Chromium 136, 1920x1080, tested with fill("A".repeat(2000))

Screenshot Evidence

edge-long-input.png — captured 2026-04-21
BUG-006
Average page load exceeds 10 seconds across every top-level page
MEDIUM

Page

All pages

Steps to Reproduce

  1. Open a fresh browser session (no cache)
  2. Log in as admin user
  3. Navigate to each top-level page in the sidebar (Dashboard, Tasks, Work Orders, Assets, Issues, etc.)
  4. Measure time from navigation start to Playwright's `networkidle` event

Expected Behavior

Pages should load within 2–4 seconds. Anything over 5 seconds is a UX problem; 10+ seconds is a serious performance issue.

Actual Behavior

Crawl of 21 pages showed an AVERAGE load time of 10,997ms (~11 seconds). Every single page — including pages with zero rows like Commitments and Work Order Planning — took at least 10 seconds to reach network-idle. Slowest pages: Scheduling 13.9s, Assets 11.8s, Attachments 11.3s. Even pages with no data loaded (0 rows) still took 10+ seconds. This suggests the slowness is not "data volume" — it's a structural issue (serial waterfall of API calls, large JS bundle, long critical rendering path).

Impact

Users face ~11 seconds of blank/loading screen on every navigation. Compounded across a typical workflow (Dashboard → Tasks → Asset → Issue → back), that is 40–50 seconds of wait per workflow. This significantly degrades productivity and makes the platform feel broken even when it is working.

Suggested Fix

Profile the network waterfall (DevTools > Network). Common fixes: (1) parallelize independent API calls instead of serial await chains, (2) lazy-load non-critical widgets, (3) add React.lazy() / code-splitting for large route bundles, (4) cache company/user data globally (it does not change per-page). Set a target budget of 2s p95 for navigation and measure in CI.

Environment

V1.21, Chromium 136, 1920x1080, 21-page crawl 2026-04-20, non-headless, no cache priming

Screenshot Evidence

crawl-dashboard.png — captured 2026-04-21
BUG-007
Auth cookies (access_token, refresh_token) use SameSite=None — form-POST CSRF surface (CORS blocks JSON fetches but not form POSTs)
MEDIUM

Page

All authenticated pages

Steps to Reproduce

  1. Log in
  2. Open DevTools Application tab > Cookies > https://acme.qa.egalvanic.ai
  3. Inspect access_token and refresh_token rows — both show SameSite=None
  4. Also inspect /api/auth/login response headers: Access-Control-Allow-Origin is explicitly set to https://acme.qa.egalvanic.ai (strict CORS) with Access-Control-Allow-Credentials: true

Expected Behavior

Auth cookies should use SameSite=Lax (default modern safe choice) or SameSite=Strict. SameSite=None is only required when the cookie must be sent to a different site in a cross-site context (e.g., embedded widget, multi-domain SSO). When SameSite=None is used, CSRF protection must be provided by Origin/Referer header validation or an explicit CSRF token on state-changing requests.

Actual Behavior

Both access_token and refresh_token have SameSite=None. They ARE HttpOnly and Secure (good). Deep re-verification on 2026-04-21 confirmed via raw Set-Cookie headers: "refresh_token=...; Domain=egalvanic.ai; Secure; HttpOnly; Path=/api/; SameSite=None" and same for access_token. ALSO verified the login response sets Access-Control-Allow-Origin: https://acme.qa.egalvanic.ai with Allow-Credentials: true — so cross-origin fetch() with JSON cannot READ responses (CORS blocks). However, this does NOT fully mitigate CSRF: (1) Plain

POST submissions from attacker sites DO send the cookies and DO execute the state change even if the attacker cannot read the response, (2) Any endpoint accepting application/x-www-form-urlencoded, multipart/form-data, or text/plain is exposed. No CSRF token was observed in any API request.

Impact

MEDIUM. CORS partially mitigates — malicious-origin JSON fetches cannot read responses so "read" attacks are blocked. But "write" CSRF still works via . If any authenticated endpoint accepts form-encoded bodies (common for multipart file uploads), a user who visits a malicious page while logged in could unknowingly trigger an action in eGalvanic. For an electrical-safety tracking app, that could mean unauthorized issue creation/deletion, asset manipulation, or file upload.

Suggested Fix

Preferred: set SameSite=Lax on both cookies. Since the SPA and API are same-origin (both on acme.qa.egalvanic.ai), Lax covers all legitimate flows and blocks cross-site POST attacks — this is the cheapest and safest fix. If cross-subdomain cookie sharing is required later (e.g., admin.egalvanic.ai), Lax still allows same-site navigation. If SameSite=None must remain (e.g., for embedded widgets or future cross-origin SSO), then add either: (a) a double-submit CSRF token header validated server-side on every non-GET request, or (b) strict Origin/Referer header validation rejecting requests from non-egalvanic.ai origins on all state-changing endpoints (also ensure no endpoint accepts non-JSON content types without CSRF protection).

Environment

V1.21. Cookie: access_token (Cognito JWT RS256, TTL 3600s/60min), refresh_token (JWE encrypted). Both domain=egalvanic.ai, path=/api/, HttpOnly+Secure+SameSite=None. CORS: Access-Control-Allow-Origin: https://acme.qa.egalvanic.ai, Allow-Credentials: true. Evidence: cookies.json, cookies-deep.json, raw Set-Cookie capture.

Screenshot Evidence

bug016-cookies.png — captured 2026-04-21
BUG-008
No rate limiting on /api/auth/login — 28 consecutive failed logins (incl. 8 against real admin email) all return 401 with zero throttling, zero CAPTCHA, zero lockout
HIGH

Page

/login (API: POST /api/auth/login)

Steps to Reproduce

  1. Open /login OR hit POST /api/auth/login directly
  2. Submit N failed logins back-to-back across 3 patterns: (A) 10 different non-existent emails, (B) 10 failures against one same non-existent email, (C) 8 failures against the REAL admin email abhiyant.singh+admin@egalvanic.com with wrong passwords
  3. Observe response statuses, Retry-After headers, X-RateLimit-* headers, and response body

Expected Behavior

After N (typically 3-5) failed attempts from the same IP or against the same email, the server should: (a) return 429 Too Many Requests with a Retry-After header, (b) require a CAPTCHA challenge, and/or (c) temporarily lock the target account (Cognito supports this via "Temporary user lockouts" policy). Industry best practice: per-IP + per-account sliding-window limiter plus CAPTCHA after 3 failures.

Actual Behavior

Deep re-verification on 2026-04-21 via direct node https calls: 28 total attempts across 3 patterns — Pattern A (10 different emails), Pattern B (10 same non-existent email), Pattern C (8 failures against REAL admin email). ALL 28 attempts returned HTTP 401 with body {"error":"Invalid credentials"}. Zero 429 responses. Zero Retry-After headers. Zero X-RateLimit-* headers. Zero progressive delay (latencies 378-1245ms, effectively random). Zero account lockout on the real admin email even after 8 consecutive failures. The endpoint will accept thousands of attempts per minute.

Impact

HIGH. Enables real exploitation paths: (1) Credential-stuffing — test leaked username/password lists from public breach dumps (haveibeenpwned-scale) against real eGalvanic accounts at high throughput. (2) Targeted brute-force — against a known admin email (e.g., discoverable from LinkedIn or auto-generated pattern), cycle through common/leaked passwords. Verified: 8 failures against the real admin email produced no lockout, so an attacker can continue attempts indefinitely. (3) Denial-of-service — flood /api/auth/login with failures to drive auth-service load or fill Cognito quota. For an electrical-safety tracking platform, a compromised admin account means access to all sites, all assets, all users, and the ability to delete compliance records. This is ELEVATED from the original MEDIUM rating because deep verification against the real admin email confirmed no account lockout — a specific and exploitable weakness, not a theoretical one.

Suggested Fix

Add rate limiting at one or more of: (1) AWS WAF on the CloudFront distribution — rule: /api/auth/login >5/min per source IP returns 429 with Retry-After. (2) AWS Cognito "Adaptive authentication" or "User pool policies > Password reuse and temporary user lockouts" — Cognito can lock after N failures automatically. (3) Application-level middleware (express-rate-limit / fastapi-limiter) keyed on IP + attempted email with sliding window. (4) After 3 failures from an IP, require CAPTCHA (hCaptcha or Cloudflare Turnstile) to distinguish humans from automated attempts. Pair with Sentry/CloudWatch alerting on sustained 401 bursts.

Environment

V1.21. Probe 2026-04-21 via node https module (3 patterns, 28 attempts total). All 28 returned 401 with no rate-limit artifacts. Evidence: rate-limit-deep.json (28 attempt records), rate-limit-6.json (earlier 6-attempt probe), bug017-ratelimit.png. Severity elevated from MEDIUM to HIGH after deep verification against real admin email.

Screenshot Evidence

bug017-ratelimit.png — captured 2026-04-21

Captured across 12 pages during Part 3 testing session (140 total console errors, 20 API errors)

Category Count Unique Errors Impact

PLuG SDK (DevRev support widget) 17 1 unique error See BUG-003 — Support widget non-functional
Resource Load Failures (HTTP 429) 16 1 unique (Beamer tracking rate-limited) Beamer analytics being rate-limited. Low impact.
HTTP Errors (401/400) 4 2 unique (auth/me 401, auth/refresh 400) Initial auth negotiation. Expected during login flow.

Pages Verified as Working Correctly

The following pages were interactively tested and found to be functioning as expected:

  • Site Overview (Dashboard) — All 6 stat cards load correctly (Assets 21, Issues 9, Tasks 7, WOs 16, Opps $24.2k, Equipment at Risk $295.0k). Pie charts render. Site selector works.
  • Tasks — CRUD works: list displays, Add Task panel opens with proper fields (Task Type, Asset, Procedure, Title, Description, Due Date, Photos), form validation catches required fields, task creation succeeds.
  • Assets — 21 assets listed, click-through to detail works, detail shows Core Attributes/OCP/Connections/Issues/Tasks/Photos/Attachments tabs. Search input functional.
  • Arc Flash Readiness — Overview shows progress gauges (70% Asset Details, 8% Connections, 29% Connection Details, 0% Eng Approved). Per-type breakdown: Busway 100%, Circuit Breaker 97%, Fuse 0%, Generator 100%. Tabs work.
  • Scheduling — Calendar renders with Quarter/Month/Week/Day views, all view toggles work, Today/Back/Next navigation works. Shows WO items.
  • Admin Settings — Sites tab loads with correct columns and "Create Site" button. Users/Classes/PM/Forms tabs present.
  • Connections — Lists 2 rows, "Add" button present.
  • Opportunities — Create form opens correctly with auto-filled Facility and required Opportunity Name field.
  • Panel Schedules, Commitments, Equipment Library, Condition Assessment, Work Order Planning — All render content and navigation works.

Generated by Playwright automated deep testing • Report by abhiyant.singh@egalvanic.com • April 20, 2026

10 test sessions + full-platform crawl + 32 PDF bugs cross-verified × 25 pages = 14 verified bugs (3 HIGH, 9 MEDIUM, 2 LOW) | Quality over quantity
